subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: A random variable has a Pareto distribution with parameters $\alpha > 0$ and $x_0 > 0$ if its density function has the form $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{\alpha x_0^\alpha}{x^{\alpha+1}} & x > x_0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ (a) Show that $f(x)$ is indeed a density function. (b) Find $E(X)$ and $\mathrm{Var}(X)$.
explanation: (a) By definition $f(x) > 0$. Also, $\int_{x_0}^\infty f(x)\,dx = \int_{x_0}^\infty \frac{\alpha x_0^\alpha}{x^{\alpha+1}}\,dx = -\left(\frac{x_0}{x}\right)^\alpha\Big|_{x_0}^\infty = 1$. (b) We have $E(X) = \int_{x_0}^\infty x f(x)\,dx = \int_{x_0}^\infty \frac{\alpha x_0^\alpha}{x^\alpha}\,dx = \frac{\alpha}{1-\alpha}\left(\frac{x_0^\alpha}{x^{\alpha-1}}\right)\Big|_{x_0}^\infty = \frac{\alpha x_0}{\alpha - 1}$ provided $\alpha > 1$. Similarly, $E(X^2) = \int_{x_0}^\infty x^2 f(x)\,dx = \int_{x_0}^\infty \frac{\alpha x_0^\alpha}{x^{\alpha-1}}\,dx = \frac{\alpha}{2-\alpha}\left(\frac{x_0^\alpha}{x^{\alpha-2}}\right)\Big|_{x_0}^\infty = \frac{\alpha x_0^2}{\alpha - 2}$ provided $\alpha > 2$. Hence, $\mathrm{Var}(X) = \frac{\alpha x_0^2}{\alpha - 2} - \frac{\alpha^2 x_0^2}{(\alpha - 1)^2} = \frac{\alpha x_0^2}{(\alpha - 2)(\alpha - 1)^2}$
